name: schema
context: Define Drizzle ORM tables, columns, indexes, enums, and constraints in TypeScript using pgTable/mysqlTable/sqliteTable
info: Drizzle schemas are plain TypeScript — no custom language, no code generation. You define tables using dialect-specific functions (pgTable, mysqlTable, sqliteTable) with columns, constraints, and indexes. Column types map directly to database types: serial, integer, text, varchar, boolean, timestamp, uuid, json/jsonb, numeric, bigint. Constraints are chained: .primaryKey(), .notNull(), .unique(), .default(), .references(). Indexes and composite keys are defined in a third argument callback. Enums use pgEnum for PostgreSQL or inline for MySQL. Schema files are the source of truth for drizzle-kit migrations — every change to a schema file can be detected and turned into SQL via [[drizzle/migrate]]. Organize schemas in a single file or split across multiple files. See [[drizzle/relations]] for application-level relation definitions and [[drizzle/config]] for pointing drizzle-kit at your schema files.
triggers: add columns to a drizzle table
define a foreign key in drizzle

sections.title: Table definition patterns
sections.body: Use `pgTable('name', columns)` for PostgreSQL, `mysqlTable('name', columns)` for MySQL, `sqliteTable('name', columns)` for SQLite. Columns are functions from the dialect's core module (e.g., `drizzle-orm/pg-core`). Chain constraints: `.primaryKey()`, `.notNull()`, `.unique()`, `.default(value)`, `.references(() => otherTable.column)`. A third argument callback defines indexes and composite keys.

sections.title: Column types reference
sections.body: PostgreSQL: `serial`, `integer`, `bigint`, `text`, `varchar`, `boolean`, `timestamp`, `date`, `uuid`, `json`, `jsonb`, `numeric`, `real`, `doublePrecision`, `bytea`. MySQL: `serial`, `int`, `bigint`, `tinyint`, `varchar`, `text`, `boolean`, `timestamp`, `datetime`, `json`, `decimal`, `float`, `double`, `binary`. SQLite: `integer`, `text`, `real`, `blob`. Use `$type<T>()` on any column to override the TypeScript type.

sections.title: Constraints and references
sections.body: `.primaryKey()` sets the primary key. `.notNull()` adds NOT NULL. `.unique()` adds a UNIQUE constraint. `.default(value)` sets a default (use `defaultNow()` for timestamps). `.references(() => table.column)` creates a foreign key — pass `{ onDelete: 'cascade' }` for delete behavior. Options: `cascade`, `restrict`, `no action`, `set null`, `set default`. Use `primaryKey({ columns: [a, b] })` in the third arg for composite PKs.

sections.title: Indexes
sections.body: Define indexes in the third argument callback: `(table) => [index('name').on(table.col), uniqueIndex('name').on(table.col)]`. Composite indexes: `index('name').on(table.col1, table.col2)`. Indexes improve query performance on columns used in WHERE, ORDER BY, and JOIN clauses. Always index foreign key columns.

sections.title: Enums
sections.body: PostgreSQL: `export const role = pgEnum('role', ['user', 'admin'])` then `role('role').default('user')` in the table. MySQL: `mysqlEnum('role', ['user', 'admin'])` inline. SQLite: use `text().$type<'user' | 'admin'>()` since SQLite has no native enum. Enums are type-safe — TypeScript will error if you pass an invalid value.

sections.title: Type inference
sections.body: Use `typeof users.$inferSelect` for the shape returned by queries (all fields, nullable where optional). Use `typeof users.$inferInsert` for the shape accepted by inserts (omits auto-generated fields like serial IDs and defaulted timestamps). These types stay in sync with your schema automatically — no code generation step needed.

related.note: After changing the schema, run drizzle-kit generate to create migrations
Relations are defined separately from tables but reference schema columns
Schema tables are passed to db.select().from() and db.insert()
Prisma uses a custom .prisma schema language; Drizzle uses plain TypeScript

scripts.name: Define Drizzle Schema
scripts.description: Generate a Drizzle schema file with tables, columns, indexes, and enums for PostgreSQL, MySQL, or SQLite

DRIZZLE_DIALECT.description: Database dialect: postgresql, mysql, or sqlite

schemaPath.description: Path to the generated schema file

depends.reason: drizzle-kit needs a config file pointing to the schema for migration generation
